top_signals.title: Odd Lots: The Hidden Plumbing of Commodity Finance (Podcast)
top_signals.summary: We talk about the commodity supply chain all the time. We talk about the ports and the trucks and the ships and all of that. But there’s another dimension to moving commodities all around the world, which is actually paying for it. Who funds the oil tanker and what happens when that tanker is, say, stuck in the Strait of Hormuz? Commodity finance underpins production, transportation and storage of a wide variety of the things that make the modern world, but you tend to only hear about it when th
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Strategy's Michael Saylor teases BTC buy with 'working better' tweet
top_signals.summary: Strategy may be set to announce it has resumed buying Bitcoin after pausing in recent weeks as the biggest crypto looks poised to close out the month down more than 3.5%.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: The Hidden Plumbing of Commodity Finance
top_signals.summary: Who pays to keep stuff moving in an age of disruptions?

top_signals.title: KBRA Releases Research – European Fibre ABS: From Build-out to Securitisation
top_signals.summary: KBRA releases research examining how European fibre assets may fit within an ABS framework. The US fibre ABS market provides the clearest securitisation reference point, while European fibre financings offer useful context on operating performance and contractual revenue models. As of May 2026, KBRA had rated approximately $29.9 billion of US fibre ABS and rated [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
